body:
## Verdict (reconciled 2026-05-28)

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: incapable — pi writes completed messages atomically; no partial-flush trace in the rollout the daemon tails
driver_capability: ready

The prior daemon_capability:full was a code-read inference assuming pi flushes partial assistant content to the JSONL as it streams. A live recording disproved the premise: a multi-line reply lands as a single stopReason=stop assistant line, and sibling pi transcripts (basic-turn, multi-turn-conversation) show one consolidated line per turn. Streamed partial content lives only in the terminal UI, which the daemon never sees.

---

## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

`streaming-partial-writes` (scenario-meanings.md): the assistant message arrives in multiple partial flushes before the turn-end signal; state must stay `working` across all flushes with no premature `→ ready`. User-observable signal: `ready → working → ready`, with `working` sustained across every partial flush — no flicker to `ready` before the turn-end signal arrives. Primitive `streaming_output`; the irrlicht primitive is that partial flushes do NOT trigger a `→ ready` transition. Requires `headless_mode`.

**agent_supports: yes.** Pi streams assistant output incrementally and writes assistant content to the JSONL rollout as it goes. A prompt that elicits a longer reply (e.g. a multi-line haiku or a numbered list of facts) produces several assistant transcript writes within one turn before the final assistant message lands.

**daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready.** Pi's turn boundary is defined ONLY by `stopReason == "stop"`. The pi parser (parser.go:78-83) maps an assistant message with `stopReason == "stop"` to `turn_done` (the working→ready settle) and EVERY other assistant message — including non-terminal partial/streamed flushes — to `assistant` (mid-turn), which sustains `working` and is excluded from IsAgentDone(). So streamed/partial assistant writes can never cause a spurious `→ ready` flap: the turn is one contiguous `working → ready` arc. The compaction-interspersing risk noted in the stale coverage cell (`daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready`) does NOT apply: pi emits `compaction` as a top-level event that the parser maps to `assistant` (parser.go:55-60), which ALSO sustains `working` (promotes ready→working) rather than settling to ready — so even a mid-stream compaction cannot flicker the state. That is why this reassessment lifts the verdict to yes/yes.

**Confidence 0.72** (not higher): you cannot deterministically force a torn/partial JSONL line from a live CLI — the assertion here is about state STABILITY across streamed content (one contiguous working→ready arc, no intermediate ready/working flap), not a hand-crafted oversized/torn line (that is the separate oversized-transcript-line cell). The prompt pins a longer reply so streaming clearly happens, but the exact number of intermediate flushes is model-dependent; only the state topology (single sustained working span, no waiting, one process_exited) is asserted and is robust regardless of flush count.

## Recipe-shape note (SCRIPT / interactive)

Recorded with the INTERACTIVE pi driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh, a tmux REPL) rather than headless `pi --print`. WHY (concern C6): headless `pi --print` exits the instant it emits the final reply, so the daemon's scanner can miss the terminal working→ready settle before process_exited; script-mode keeps pi alive across one send + wait_turn so the daemon observes the settle cleanly, then a single tmux teardown yields one clean process_exited. The prompt deliberately elicits a LONGER reply (a 6-line haiku, one line at a time) so multiple assistant flushes stream before the turn-end stop. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session. The streaming turn arc, pid_bind and teardown all pin to the rollout session via the first-working anchor (NO session pin on turn_start, so the matcher binds the FIRST working, necessarily the rollout session; the pre-session never goes working) + same_session_as. The phase DSL has no per-flush form, so the no-flicker property is asserted as a single sustained working span: exactly ONE working→ready arc with a no-state_transition-to-ready invariant across the streaming window, plus a no-waiting invariant.
caveats: RECONCILED 2026-05-28 (consistency-gate): daemon_capability full→incapable. A live recording proved pi writes each completed message as ONE consolidated rollout line (stopReason=stop) with NO intermediate partial-assistant flushes — incremental streaming is terminal-UI only and never reaches the JSONL the daemon tails. The canonical streaming-partial-writes signal (state held across multiple partial flushes) therefore leaves no observable trace. Viewer now renders orange/unobservable; scenarios.json by_adapter.pi applicable:false agrees.

The whole streamed turn is a SINGLE working→ready arc: pi sustains working across every partial/streamed assistant flush — only the final assistant message with stopReason=stop ends the turn. The spec asserts exactly one turn_end ready, no intermediate ready flap (streaming-window invariant), no waiting.

Pi maps a mid-stream `compaction` top-level event to assistant (sustains working), so the stale coverage caveat 'compaction event interspersing risks flicker' does not hold — this is why the verdict is reassessed to yes/yes rather than the coverage cell's irrlicht_observes:partial.
Pi cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID — it generates one itself; the interactive driver discovers the UUID from the session header on line 1 of the freshly-created rollout transcript.

Because the live REPL bridges with a transient proc-<PID> pre-session, the turn phase anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher binds the FIRST working (necessarily the rollout session; the pre-session never goes working); every later phase pins to it via same_session_as.

Confidence 0.72: a live CLI cannot be forced to emit a torn/partial JSONL line on demand (that is the oversized-transcript-line cell); the property asserted is state stability across streamed content, and the exact flush count is model-dependent — only the state topology is asserted.

sources.note: parser.go:78-83 — assistant message with stopReason==stop → turn_done (working→ready); EVERY other assistant message (incl. streamed/partial flushes) → assistant (mid-turn), sustains working, excluded from IsAgentDone(). parser.go:55-60 — top-level `compaction` event → assistant (sustains working), so a mid-stream compaction cannot flicker to ready.

Sibling pi cell confirming the parser sustains working across intermediate assistant transcript writes and settles only on stopReason=stop; events arc is ready→working→ready with no waiting transition.
streaming-partial-writes: state stays working across all partial flushes — no flicker to ready before the turn-end signal arrives; final state ready.
Interactive tmux REPL driver; supports send / wait_turn / interrupt / sleep, keeps the rollout alive so the daemon observes the working→ready settle before teardown (concern C6).
One pi turn = a single assistant line with stopReason=stop; no intermediate partial-assistant flushes — confirms pi writes completed messages atomically (partial-flush streaming is unobservable).
